title: Grader (on-disk form)
description: Schema for one grader YAML file under .tessary/graders/. The authoritative validator is validate.py (at the plugin root), which enforces these structural rules plus cross-field invariants and optional pipeline.yaml / bundle cross-references. This schema is the contract any grader author must satisfy; validate.py is the enforcer. v2 adds: self_tests[].category (self_tests removed in v7 — see below), applies_when_check (deterministic graders), per-grader runtime fields (owner, block_on_fail, cost/latency budgets, dataset_refs), and a _meta provenance block for survivable re-runs. v3 adds kind=score (continuous 1-5 quality scoring). v4 adds scope=trace (multi-turn: grade the final turn given prior n-1 messages) and kind=agentic (binary verdict produced by an agent running in a sandbox via agent_spec; emitted here, executed by the runner). v5 adds no grader fields (the change is the call-site default_grade_mode flag plus pinned trace history sourcing). v6: applies_when is ALWAYS evaluated by an LLM at runtime — for kind=deterministic the platform runs a separate LLM applicability gate before the (gate-free) deterministic_check; for llm_judge/score it rides inline in the judge prompt. applies_when_check is REMOVED (the gate is never compiled). Trace deterministic checks receive structured input (messages + tool_uses). v7 REMOVES per-grader self_tests entirely: graders no longer carry hand-authored self-test cases, the self_test_pass_rate/self_test_variance calibration fields, or the applies_when ↔ not_applicable self-test invariant. A grader's behavior is now validated platform-side against reusable golden datasets — real spans associated with the grader and labeled per (grader, dataset_item) — so the author emits only the verdict body, applies_when, confidence, and rationale. v8 DEFERS judge-prompt authoring to the platform for kind=llm_judge and kind=score: the author emits the definition (kind, applies_when, rubric_levels+score_scale for score, confidence, rationale) plus a top-level marker `_body_source: platform` and does NOT author judge_prompt/rubric; the platform expands the verdict body on import. When `_body_source` is absent the v7 requirements still hold (so deterministic/execution/agentic bodies and any legacy llm_judge/score files keep validating). v8 also adds the orchestrator-owned call-site field expected_spans (telemetry nomenclature extracted from the call site's code) — schema'd on the call_site shard, not on the grader. The $id tracks the current contract version (8).

id.description: Stable grader ID. For single_call and trace: `<failure_mode_id>::grader`. For chain: `<chain_id>::<failure_name>::grader`. Attached by orchestrator, not author.

scope.description: single_call: grades one call's output. chain: grades a relationship across N distinct call-site outputs in a session. trace: grades the final turn of a multi-turn session given the prior n-1 messages as context — anchored to one call_site_id like single_call.

failure_mode_id.description: Required for failure-catching kinds (llm_judge, deterministic, execution). The failure mode this grader catches.

quality_dimension_id.description: Required for kind=score. The quality dimension this grader scores (parallel to failure_mode_id). Resolves to a pipeline quality_dimensions[].id; validate.py --bundle enforces the bijection.

call_site_id.description: Required and non-empty when scope=single_call or scope=trace; must be null/absent when scope=chain.

chain_id.description: Required and non-empty when scope=chain; must be null/absent when scope=single_call.

taxonomy_node_id.description: Attached by orchestrator at the taxonomy step, before grader synthesis fan-out, so subagents can splice it onto each grader at write time. Required for failure-catching kinds; absent for kind=score (quality dimensions are not taxonomy-clustered).

agent_spec.description: Required for kind=agentic. Declares an agent that produces the verdict by multi-turn analysis in a sandbox (e.g. running `git diff` / tests / file exploration) rather than a single judge call. This plugin only emits the spec; the runner (evals-platform) executes it — validate.py never runs it.

harness.description: The agent harness the runner launches. Extensible enum; opencode is the only value today.

sandbox.description: The sandbox the runner provisions for the agent.

network.description: Network policy. Default to the most restrictive the task allows.

allowed_tools.description: Tools the grading agent may use, e.g. ['bash', 'read', 'git'].

task_prompt.description: The grading task the agent performs. Self-contained instructions ending in a single decision, e.g. 'Run `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` and decide whether it correctly implements the requested change.'

verdict_contract.description: How the agent must emit its result so the runner can parse it, e.g. 'print PASS or FAIL on the final line'. v1 verdicts are binary (pass/fail).

score_scale.description: Required for kind=score. The integer range the judge assigns; current convention is min 1, max 5.

rubric_levels.description: Required for kind=score. Maps each level (stringified integer in [min, max]) to a concrete, observable anchor describing output at that level. (For kind=llm_judge the prose `rubric` string is used instead.)

applies_when.description: Natural-language applicability predicate, ALWAYS evaluated by an LLM at runtime (v6). For kind=llm_judge/score it rides inline in the judge prompt; for kind=deterministic the platform runs a separate LLM applicability gate BEFORE the deterministic_check and skips out-of-scope outputs — so the deterministic_check must NOT implement the gate itself. When set, out-of-scope outputs are reported not_applicable at runtime; golden dataset items that fall outside scope are surfaced as not_applicable by the platform runner rather than asserted by the author (v7 removed per-grader self-tests).

applies_when_check.description: REMOVED in v6 — do not emit. Formerly the code-evaluable mirror of applies_when, compiled into a deterministic body. The gate is now always LLM-evaluated, so this is no longer authored or consumed; the property is retained only so older grader files still validate.

_body_source.description: v8 marker. When set to 'platform' on a kind=llm_judge or kind=score grader, the author has DEFERRED the verdict body (judge_prompt / rubric) to the platform, which expands it on import. With this marker the author emits only the definition — kind, applies_when (llm_judge), rubric_levels+score_scale (score), confidence, rationale — and MUST NOT carry a non-empty judge_prompt/rubric. Absent means the body is plugin-authored inline (the v7 contract); deterministic/execution/agentic bodies never set this and stay plugin-authored.

judge_prompt.description: Required and non-empty when kind=llm_judge — UNLESS _body_source == 'platform' (v8), in which case it is deferred to the platform and must be omitted/empty.

rubric.description: Required and non-empty when kind=llm_judge — UNLESS _body_source == 'platform' (v8), in which case it is deferred to the platform and must be omitted/empty.

confidence.description: Author-assigned confidence in this grader. Behavioral calibration now happens platform-side against golden datasets (real labeled spans), not via per-grader self-tests.

rationale.description: One sentence in user-impact terms for the curation report.

owner.description: Optional. Free-text owner or team handle responsible for this grader (e.g. '@oncall-ml', 'platform-team'). Surfaced in the viewer and report; not load-bearing.

block_on_fail.description: Optional override for the global severity_policy (in pipeline.yaml runtime.severity_policy). When true, a failing grader blocks CI/release regardless of severity. When false, a failing grader is report-only. When absent, the severity_policy default applies.

cost_budget_tokens.description: Optional. Soft budget (input + output tokens) for one judge invocation. Runtime warns when exceeded.

latency_budget_ms_p95.description: Optional. Soft p95 latency budget for one judge invocation. Runtime warns when exceeded.

dataset_refs.description: Optional. Pointers to real inputs this grader should run against — captured trace span IDs or file:line source locations. Populated by orchestrator from Path A trace ingestion or Path B static discovery. Empty/absent means 'no captured dataset'.

pack_ids.description: Set of pack ids this grader's failure mode belongs to (propagated from failure_modes[].pack_ids). Empty / absent = baseline (not pack-contributed). Each entry must resolve to a pipeline.packs[].id; validate.py --bundle enforces.

compliance_tags.description: Set of control IDs this grader maps to (e.g. 'EU-AI-Act.Art-13', 'HIPAA-164.514', 'NIST-AI-RMF.MS-2.6'). Free-form strings — no enum check — so consumers can adopt their own GRC framework IDs. Propagated from failure_modes[].compliance_tags.

_meta.description: Provenance + lock metadata used to survive re-runs. Orchestrator writes this on first emission; carries forward across re-syntheses; respects locked_fields on overwrite.

_validation_error.description: Set by the step-6 subagent if validation kept failing after 3 retries. Presence of this key is an explicit accepted-broken signal — validate.py short-circuits all other rules and returns 0 when this key is present, so the file is not re-flagged by later --pipeline cross-check passes. The operator-facing report.md and the failed-validation count in the summary line surface these files.

allOf.comment: trace grades the final turn of a multi-turn session at one call site; routes like single_call.
llm_judge requires an inline judge_prompt+rubric UNLESS the body is deferred to the platform (_body_source == 'platform', v8).
agentic produces a binary verdict via an agent running in a sandbox; the spec is emitted here, executed by the runner.
Failure-catching kinds carry a failure_mode_id and a taxonomy_node_id.
Score graders always link to a quality dimension and carry an anchored rubric + scale.
A score grader carries an inline judge_prompt UNLESS the body is deferred to the platform (_body_source == 'platform', v8).

dataset_ref.description: One pointer to a real input this grader can be run against.

jsonl_path.description: Path (relative to .tessary/) of a JSONL dataset capturing inputs for this call site, e.g. 'datasets/<call_site_id>.jsonl'.

meta.description: Provenance + lock metadata. Allows re-runs to carry hand-edits forward.

author.description: Identifier of the grader author that produced this file (e.g. 'default', 'evals-prompt').

author_contract_version.description: Contract version the author conformed to. Set by the orchestrator to the version the producing author declared (the bundled default author and evals-prompt both declare 8, the current contract version).

synthesized_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when this file was synthesized.

synth_inputs_digest.description: Hex digest (first 16+ chars of SHA-256) over the canonical author input: failure_mode + call_site/chain + product_profile snapshot. Re-runs that produce the same digest may safely overwrite locked_fields=[] graders without diff churn.

locked_fields.description: Fields the orchestrator must preserve verbatim on re-run. Set by humans during curation (or by --lock at the CLI). On overwrite, the orchestrator loads the existing file, retains values for every key in this list, and only refreshes other fields.

human_edited.description: Set to true by curators (or by tooling) to mark the file as touched. Re-runs warn before overwriting human_edited files unless --force.
